en.extract:
The moustached barbet (Psilopogon incognitus) is an Asian barbet.  Barbets are a group of near passerine birds with a world-wide tropical distribution. They get their name from the bristles which fringe their heavy bills.
The moustached barbet is a resident breeder in the hills of Myanmar, Thailand, Cambodia, Laos and  Vietnam. It is a species of broadleaf evergreen forest from 600 to 700 m. It nests in a tree hole.

This barbet is 23 cm in length. It is a plump bird, with a short neck, large head and short tail. The bill is dark horn, and the body plumage is green. The adult of the form, P. i. elbeli, which occurs in northern Thailand has a red forehead, green crown with a red spot at the rear, a blue face and throat, and black eyestripe and moustache. The sexes are similar, but the juvenile has a duller, greener head and throat with a narrower moustache..
The other subspecies are nominate P. i. incognitus in Myanmar and western Thailand, and P. i. eurous in east Thailand, Cambodia,  Laos and Vietnam.
The male's territorial call is a repeated loud u'ik-a-ruk u'ik-a-ruk u'ik-a-ruk.


References

Birdlife International
Robson, Craig  A Field Guide to the Birds of Thailand ISBN 1-84330-921-1
Ripley, The Barbets, The Auk Vol 62 1945


External links
Image at ADW

{{Short description|Species of bird}} {{speciesbox | image = CyanopsIncognita.jpg | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = {{cite iucn |author=BirdLife International |date=2016 |title=''Psilopogon incognitus'' |volume=2016 |article-number=e.T22681646A92915252 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2016-3.RLTS.T22681646A92915252.en |access-date=20 November 2021}} | genus = Psilopogon | species = incognitus | authority = ([[Allan Octavian Hume|Hume]], 1874) | synonyms = ''Cyanops incognita''
''Megalaima incognitus'' }} The '''moustached barbet''' ('''''Psilopogon incognitus''''') is an [[Megalaimidae|Asian barbet]]. Barbets are a group of [[near passerine]] [[bird]]s with a world-wide tropical distribution. They get their name from the bristles which fringe their heavy bills. The moustached barbet is a resident breeder in the hills of [[Myanmar]], [[Thailand]], [[Cambodia]], [[Laos]] and [[Vietnam]]. It is a species of broadleaf evergreen forest from 600 to 700 m. It nests in a tree hole. [[File:CyanopsSmit.jpg|thumb|upright|left|Head (bottom), illustration by [[Joseph Smit]], 1891]] This barbet is 23 cm in length. It is a plump bird, with a short neck, large head and short tail. The bill is dark horn, and the body [[Feather|plumage]] is green. The adult of the form, ''P. i. elbeli'', which occurs in northern Thailand has a red forehead, green crown with a red spot at the rear, a blue face and throat, and black eyestripe and moustache. The sexes are similar, but the juvenile has a duller, greener head and throat with a narrower moustache.. The other subspecies are nominate ''P. i. incognitus'' in Myanmar and western Thailand, and ''P. i. eurous'' in east Thailand, Cambodia, Laos and Vietnam. The male's territorial call is a repeated loud ''u'ik-a-ruk u'ik-a-ruk u'ik-a-ruk''. File:Megalaima incognita - moustached barbet juvenile.jpg|Juvenile, [[Khao Yai National Park]] / Thailand ==References== {{Reflist}} *[http://www.birdlife.org/datazone/species/index.html?action=SpcHTMDetails.asp&sid=772&m=0 '''Birdlife International'''] *Robson, Craig ''A Field Guide to the Birds of Thailand'' {{ISBN|1-84330-921-1}} *Ripley, The Barbets, ''The Auk'' Vol 62 1945 ==External links== *[http://animaldiversity.ummz.umich.edu/site/resources/david_behrens/MoustachedBarbet.jpg/view.html Image at ADW] {{Taxonbar|from=Q27074859}} [[Category:Psilopogon|moustached barbet]] [[Category:Birds of Cambodia]] [[Category:Birds of Laos]] [[Category:Birds of Vietnam]] [[Category:Birds of Indochina]] [[Category:Birds described in 1874|moustached barbet]]

zh.extract:
休氏拟啄木鸟（学名：Psilopogon incognitus）也作休氏拟鴷，一种分布于东南亚中南半岛地区的䴕形目鸟类，隶属于拟啄木鸟科拟啄木鸟属。本种由英国鸟类学家艾伦·奥克塔维恩·休谟于1874年描述发表。
